channel.title: NPR Topics: Business
channel.description: Find the latest business news with reports on Wall Street, interest rates, banking, companies, and U.S. and world financial markets. Subscribe to the Business Story of the Day podcast.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Gas crosses $4 a gallon in the U.S. for the first time in 3 years
item.description: The war with Iran has driven up gas prices at a time when affordability is high on people's minds.
item.content:encoded:
The war with Iran has driven up gas prices at a time when affordability is high on people's minds.

(Image credit: Ronaldo Schemidt)
item.dc:creator: Stephan Bisaha

item.title: Former Alex Jones employee says: 'It was nonsense, it was lies'
item.description: Josh Owens spent four years as a video editor and field producer for Jones' Infowars media company. "It was all about making things look cinematic," he says. Owens' memoir is The Madness of Believing.
item.content:encoded:
Josh Owens spent four years as a video editor and field producer for Jones' Infowars media company. "It was all about making things look cinematic," he says. Owens' memoir is The Madness of Believing.

(Image credit: Joe Buglewicz)
item.dc:creator: Dave Davies

item.title: How Trump's EEOC is attacking DEI and emphasizing white people
item.description: Andrea Lucas, the Trump-appointed chair of the Equal Employment Opportunity Commission, has set a new agenda for an agency that long prioritized vulnerable and underserved workers.
item.content:encoded:
Andrea Lucas, the Trump-appointed chair of the Equal Employment Opportunity Commission, has set a new agenda for an agency that long prioritized vulnerable and underserved workers.

(Image credit: Elizabeth Gillis)
item.dc:creator: Andrea Hsu

item.title: The final batch of World Cup tickets is about to go on sale. Here are 5 things to know
item.description: FIFA is kicking off its last sales for World Cup tickets on Wednesday. From prices to why FOMO is working against you, here's what you need to know.
item.content:encoded:
FIFA is kicking off its last sales for World Cup tickets on Wednesday. From prices to why FOMO is working against you, here's what you need to know.

(Image credit: Robert Cianflone)
item.dc:creator: Rafael Nam

item.title: China's AI chatbots are advanced and versatile — and begging for more users
item.description: Chinese AI companies are focused less on being cutting edge and more on attracting customers. That means holiday promotions, and making chatbots useful in everyday life.
item.content:encoded:
Chinese AI companies are focused less on being cutting edge and more on attracting customers. That means holiday promotions, and making chatbots useful in everyday life.

(Image credit: ADEK BERRY/AFP via Getty Images)
item.dc:creator: John Ruwitch

item.title: Trump says he has 'no problem' with Russian oil tanker bringing relief to Cuba despite blockade
item.description: President Donald Trump said he has "no problem" with a Russian oil tanker off the coast of Cuba delivering relief to the island, which has been brought to its knees by a U.S. oil blockade.
item.content:encoded:
President Donald Trump said he has "no problem" with a Russian oil tanker off the coast of Cuba delivering relief to the island, which has been brought to its knees by a U.S. oil blockade.

(Image credit: Ramon Espinosa)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: He wants children's bikes made in the U.S.A. — and tariffs against his rivals
item.description: Nearly all the bicycles sold in the United States are made overseas. An Indiana company set out to change that — and it's seeking a push from the Trump administration's tariffs.
item.content:encoded:
Nearly all the bicycles sold in the United States are made overseas. An Indiana company set out to change that — and it's seeking a push from the Trump administration's tariffs.

(Image credit: Scott Horsley)
item.dc:creator: Scott Horsley

item.title: Stuck in a long TSA line? Here are some strategies if you need to rebook your flight
item.description: Extreme TSA lines at airports have left many passengers scrambling to rebook flights missed due to delays. But while airlines say they're helping flyers, they're not obligated to do so.
item.content:encoded:
Extreme TSA lines at airports have left many passengers scrambling to rebook flights missed due to delays. But while airlines say they're helping flyers, they're not obligated to do so.

(Image credit: Michael M. Santiago)
item.dc:creator: Bill Chappell

item.title: Polymarket tries to woo a skeptical D.C. with a prediction market-themed bar
item.description: One of the largest prediction market platforms, Polymarket, opened a pop-up bar in the heart of Washington, D.C.'s lobbying district.
item.content:encoded: One of the largest prediction market platforms, Polymarket, opened a pop-up bar in the heart of Washington, D.C.'s lobbying district.
item.dc:creator: Luke Garrett

item.title: How long will the war last? No one knows, and it's making oil prices weird
item.description: It's like the "Schrödinger's cat" thought experiment. There are two very different potential realities, and traders don't yet know which one is true.
item.content:encoded:
It's like the "Schrödinger's cat" thought experiment. There are two very different potential realities, and traders don't yet know which one is true.

(Image credit: Ahn Young-joon)
item.dc:creator: Camila Domonoske
